name: Faizeen Saba Naz
title: AI/ML Engineer / Robotics AI Developer
about: AI/ML Engineer and M.Tech Computer Science postgraduate with hands-on experience in creating, fine-tuning, evaluating, and deploying machine learning, deep learning, and NLP models. Skilled in Python, PyTorch, Transformer/BERT architectures, transfer learning, data preprocessing, model evaluation, explainable AI, and AI-enabled robotics workflows. Interested in applying AI/ML to medical imaging, Hinglish NLP, forecasting, robotics automation, and intelligent software systems.

education.degree: M.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering
B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering

education.institution: Jamia Millia Islamia, New Delhi, India

description: Created and integrated AI/ML-driven robotics modules using sensor data, preprocessing, and real-time inference workflows to improve responsiveness and decision accuracy.
Fine-tuned and optimized machine-learning pipelines for robotic system behavior, improving prediction reliability and hardware-software coordination in dynamic environments.
Deployed AI-assisted automation features into robotics applications, including model evaluation, debugging, and performance monitoring for stable operation.
Implemented control logic and real-time data processing pipelines to connect robotic hardware with intelligent software systems.

projects.title: Explainable Alzheimer MRI Classification
projects.description: Developed a multiclass Alzheimer's disease classification system from brain MRI images for NonDemented, VeryMildDemented, MildDemented, and ModerateDemented stages.
details: Developed a multiclass Alzheimer's disease classification system from brain MRI images for NonDemented, VeryMildDemented, MildDemented, and ModerateDemented stages.
Fine-tuned AlexNet, GoogLeNet, MobileNetV2, EfficientNet-B0, and ResNet50 using transfer learning with preprocessing, augmentation, and leakage verification.
Validated the models on OASIS, Kaggle held-out, and third external MRI datasets to measure cross-dataset robustness and generalization.
Achieved best results with EfficientNet-B0: 99.34% OASIS accuracy, 96.98% Kaggle held-out accuracy, and 95.60% external dataset accuracy.
Integrated Grad-CAM and saliency maps to explain model predictions and highlight disease-relevant MRI brain regions.

projects.title: Hinglish Hate Speech Detection
projects.description: Developed a Transformer-based hate speech detection system for Hinglish social media text with BERT models.
details: Developed a Transformer-based hate speech detection system for Hinglish social media text.
Fine-tuned multiple BERT variants including mBERT, XLM-RoBERTa, DistilBERT, and BERT-base.
Trained and evaluated models on approximately 76,000 labeled samples.
Achieved approximately 98% accuracy with high F1-score using multilingual BERT.
Implemented complete NLP pipeline including preprocessing, tokenization, fine-tuning, and evaluation.
Evaluated performance using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC.

projects.title: Equity Analysis & Stock Forecasting
projects.description: Designed and implemented Transformer-based architectures for stock price forecasting using attention mechanisms and financial news sentiment analysis.
details: Designed and implemented Transformer-based architectures for stock price forecasting.
Integrated OHLCV data, technical indicators, and FinBERT-based sentiment analysis from financial news.
Achieved Directional Accuracy (DA) of 90.1% and improved risk-adjusted returns.
Provided interpretable attention visualizations to identify influential market drivers.
Benchmarked models against existing approaches and demonstrated improved forecasting performance.
